mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes or OpenShift, simplifying the deployment and management of Kafka clusters. It provides a Kubernetes-native way to manage Kafka, including topics, users, and ACLs. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing Kafka configurations.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Strimzi Helm repository.
> Updates the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the 'kafka' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Kafka Operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Strimzi Kafka Operator is running correctly in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kafka' namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD) provided by Strimzi.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/0.44.0/examples/kafka/kafka-persistent.yaml

```

> Deploys a Kafka cluster with persistent storage.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kafka cluster is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> Lists all Kafka clusters in the 'kafka' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not retained.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the Kafka namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Kafka data. This step upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Backs up the current Kafka cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Kafka Operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Kafka cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the Kafka CR.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure the Kafka CR is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kafka pods are failing to start, possibly due to configuration errors.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kafka' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Kafka cluster not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Networking issues or incorrect service configurations can prevent access to the Kafka cluster.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the correct ports are open.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the Kafka cluster has not been created or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Check the Kafka CRD and ensure it has been applied correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the 'KafkaMirrorMaker' resource in favor of 'KafkaMirrorMaker2'.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.43, update your KafkaMirrorMaker resources to KafkaMirrorMaker2.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed when running 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka', and the operator pods should be in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
